title: Best Restaurants in Hua Hin Thailand (2026)
meta_description: Where to eat in Hua Hin for 2026: night-market seafood, Chat Chai breakfast, Cicada weekends, and the resort restaurants that actually fit the town.
intro: Hua Hin food is better when you do not force it into one category. The town's real strength is the mix: old-center seafood and street food, breakfast-market rhythm, curated weekend market eating, and a handful of resort restaurants that genuinely belong in the plan because Hua Hin is a resort town with real hotel-dining depth. These are the Hua Hin food stops that actually make sense.

items.name: Hua Hin Night Market seafood lanes
items.description: Still the easiest first-night dinner plan in Hua Hin if you want seafood, snacks, and town-center energy together.
highlights: Best classic Hua Hin evening food stop
Seafood and street food in one zone
items.current_info: Best after dark when both the seafood rows and the snack-led stalls are fully active.

items.name: Chat Chai Market breakfast lanes
items.description: The best morning food move in Hua Hin if you want to start the day in a working market rather than in resort mode.
highlights: Good for fruit, snacks, and simple meals
Works before the beach or a day trip
items.current_info: Go early and keep it flexible; the point is market rhythm, not a single destination dish.

items.name: Cicada and Tamarind market food zone
items.description: The strongest weekend evening answer when the group wants dinner, drinks, and browsing without a formal restaurant booking.
highlights: Easy for shared and mixed tastes
Good pairing of market atmosphere and food convenience
items.current_info: Better as one whole evening than as a rushed add-on after dinner elsewhere.

items.name: Railway Restaurant at Centara Grand Beach Resort & Villas Hua Hin
items.description: A worthwhile heritage-hotel meal if you want Hua Hin's old resort identity to be part of the dining experience.
items.location: Centara Grand Beach Resort & Villas Hua Hin
items.current_info: Best when you want one polished sit-down meal without leaving central Hua Hin behind.

items.name: McFarland House at Hyatt Regency Hua Hin
items.description: A strong beachfront resort-dining choice on the south side if the location already fits your stay.
items.current_info: Most practical when you are already staying or spending time on the Nong Kae side.

items.name: Praca at The Standard, Hua Hin
items.description: A design-led Thai dining option that fits Hua Hin's more polished hotel side without feeling disconnected from the town.
items.current_info: Better for a deliberate dinner than for travelers seeking the cheapest or most local meal of the trip.

items.name: Amber Kitchen at Hua Hin Marriott Resort & Spa
items.description: A practical all-day dining option when family logistics, weather, or resort convenience matter most.
items.current_info: Most useful for central-resort guests or travelers who want a comfort-led meal without extra planning.

items.name: Sea Fire Salt at Anantara Hua Hin Resort
items.description: A worthwhile beachfront splurge on the north side if the property or area already suits your trip.
items.current_info: This works best as one intentional resort dinner, not as the default every night.

items.name: Oceanside Beach Club & Restaurant at Putahracsa Hua Hin
items.description: A stylish near-center beachfront meal if you want one sunset-facing dining stop without committing to a full-size resort.
items.current_info: Works especially well for couples or travelers staying close to the older center.
